comment.body: I thought some on the weekend.  My analogy could be off, but there seems to be two ways of looking at greeks.  First, is what most people are familiar with, putting together a spread, basically putting on a position and understanding time decay and vol premium.  The second way, is thru the lense of a Dealer.  Having a whole book of positions and managing the book.

comment.body:
@marvin romanow  one can simply derive estimated gamma based on the reported volume data from exchanges. it is purchaseable.
the common assumption is that MMs hedge both regular and flex options, while firms only hedge regular ones. 
the difference is that regular options (listed on CBOE) have fixed strikes and expos and contract sizes, while on flex contract the customer can choose these terms. 
I always take both into account.
comment.raw_body:
${0}  one can simply derive estimated gamma based on the reported volume data from exchanges. it is purchaseable.
the common assumption is that MMs hedge both regular and flex options, while firms only hedge regular ones. 
the difference is that regular options (listed on CBOE) have fixed strikes and expos and contract sizes, while on flex contract the customer can choose these terms. 
I always take both into account.

comment.body:
ATTENTION!☝️☝️☝️
 Especially vol traders like @Trader McGraw @CMik and others...
Here you see, the smirk on SPX (I watch SPX bcs SPY relies on it too)
..
As you can see vol is pretty much bid on both wings. Even tho my stochvol models expect vol to increase, it is estimated like ~33% higher than it should be rational. 
Due to vanna, the deltas are bid pretty much on OTM contracts. 
I will short the smirk into Friday via 10-15 delta contracts now.
